analysis.period_summary: Week of March 30–April 5 generated $6,853 revenue from 117 frontend sales across 12,712 total landing page views. The most critical finding is a 6.16% checkout-to-purchase rate on the primary flow (c→offer_v4) which is handling 95% of traffic—this represents a severe conversion leak costing approximately $3,000+ in lost weekly revenue.

funnel_scorecard.commentary: Overall funnel health is compromised by a critical checkout-to-purchase leak. The primary flow (c→offer_v4) shows strong offer engagement (83% checkout rate) but catastrophic purchase conversion (6%), while a smaller split test flow (b_c→offer_v4) demonstrates 41% checkout-to-purchase—proving the leak is fixable.

funnel_leaks.why_it_matters: With 1,720 checkout clicks weekly at 6% conversion, fixing this to industry-standard 25% would add ~325 sales/week at $37 = ~$12,000 additional weekly revenue.
Offer v2 shows 58% checkout rate vs v4's 83-85%, suggesting weaker sales copy or mismatched traffic—fixing to match v4 would recover ~5 additional weekly sales from this test segment.
V3 offer shows only 36% checkout engagement despite strong 23% landing-to-optin rate, indicating fundamental offer-market fit issue worth investigating before scaling.
Landing page /a converts at 2.27% vs 14-17% on other landing pages, indicating broken page elements, wrong audience, or technical tracking error—should be killed or fixed immediately.
This test segment shows 14% optin rate vs 17% on main c→offer_v4 flow, suggesting landing page variants /b or /c underperform—opportunity to consolidate traffic to stronger variant.

upsell_analysis.commentary: Backend monetization is average with 31% OTO1 take rate (healthy) but only 13% OTO2 take rate (weak—target is 25%+). The 59 total upsell/downsell sales add $3,144 backend revenue, boosting average order value from $37 to $58.57—a 58% lift that could be improved with stronger OTO2 positioning.

needle_movers.why: The main flow c→offer_v4 has 1,720 weekly checkout clicks but only 6.16% convert to purchase, while the same offer on b_c→offer_v4 achieves 41.18% with identical checkout flow. This proves a fixable technical or payment issue is costing 600+ lost sales weekly.
what: Immediately audit ClickBank checkout integration on c→offer_v4 path for broken redirects, parameter passing errors, or session drops
Compare checkout URL structures between c→offer_v4 (broken) and b_c→offer_v4 (working 41%) to identify configuration difference
Test checkout flow personally using exact c→offer_v4 path with real card to replicate buyer experience and isolate failure point
If technical issue not found, A/B test one-click upsell vs traditional checkout button to reduce friction
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Fixing to 25% checkout-to-purchase rate = +325 sales/week × $37 = +$12,025/week, or +$625,300/year

needle_movers.why: OTO2 shows only 12.8% take rate (target is 25%+) and OTO2 downsell at 1.7% suggests broken redirect logic—buyers are not seeing the downsell after declining OTO2. With 117 frontend sales, fixing this could add 15+ upsells weekly.
what: Verify OTO2 decline redirect properly triggers downsell page—current 1.7% suggests most declines go to thank-you page instead
Review ClickBank pixel setup for OTO2 and downsell2 tracking to ensure conversions attribute correctly
Test OTO2 offer positioning and copy—12.8% is low for a $47 offer following $67 OTO1 (price anchoring should help)
Implement exit-intent popup on OTO2 decline to capture additional downsell conversions before redirect
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Increasing OTO2 combined take rate from 14.5% to 30% = +18 sales/week × $42 avg = +$756/week or +$39,312/year

needle_movers.why: Campaign 101 (c→offer_v4 path) drives 95% of traffic with 1.06% overall conversion, while Campaign 100 (b_c→offer_v4 path) achieves 3.94% conversion on same email source. The c path appears to have lower-intent traffic or page experience issues.
what: Audit Campaign 101 email creative and subject lines vs Campaign 100 to identify messaging differences affecting traffic quality
Review landing page /c/ user experience—despite 17% optin rate (healthy), the 6% checkout-to-purchase suggests disconnect between optin promise and offer delivery
Implement GA4 session recording on /c/ landing page to identify drop-off patterns or UX friction points
Consider redirecting portion of Campaign 101 traffic to proven b_c→offer_v4 path to validate whether issue is traffic quality or page experience
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Improving c→offer_v4 overall conversion from 0.87% to 2% (still below b_c→offer_v4's 4.79%) = +137 sales/week × $37 = +$5,069/week

needle_movers.why: Five active split test flows (landing→offer, landing→offer_v3, a→offer_v2, b_c→offer_v4, b_c→offer_v2) collectively generated only 17 sales from 561 views—massive data fragmentation is preventing statistically valid optimization while consuming 4% of total traffic.
what: Kill Campaigns 91, 95, 98 immediately—zero conversions on combined 130 views after sufficient time indicates fundamental failures
Consolidate Campaign 100 (b_c→offer_v4) learnings: 41% checkout-to-purchase proves offer_v4 works, but 14% landing-to-optin suggests landing variants /b or /c underperform
Archive offer_v2 and offer_v3 variants—0-3 sales on 33 total checkout clicks proves neither offers improvements worth continuing to split traffic
Redirect all cleaned-up traffic to proven c→offer_v4 path to maximize sample size for fixing critical checkout leak
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Consolidating 431 fragmented views back to main funnel at 0.87% conversion = +3.7 sales/week × $37 = +$137/week; more importantly, enables faster iteration on checkout fix

needle_movers.why: OTO1 Blueprint at $67 achieves strong 30.8% take rate, but downsell at $47 only captures 5.1% of frontend buyers (6 sales). With ~81 buyers declining OTO1, the downsell should capture 25-35%, suggesting visibility or redirect issue.
what: Verify OTO1 decline button properly redirects to downsell page—current 7% downsell take rate (6÷81 declines) is far below expected 30%+
A/B test downsell headline and offer positioning—emphasize $20 savings vs original $67 to strengthen price anchoring
Add countdown timer or scarcity element to downsell page to increase urgency and reduce abandonment
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Improving OTO1 downsell from 7% to 30% of declines = +18 sales/week × $47 = +$846/week or +$44,000/year

cpv_cb_discrepancy.note: CPV shows 123 conversions vs ClickBank 117 frontend sales—the 6-conversion difference represents OTO postback conversions, which is expected behavior and within normal reconciliation range.

analysis.data_notes: Critical data integrity concern: The b_c→offer_v4 flow shows 41.18% checkout-to-purchase rate on identical offer/checkout as c→offer_v4's 6.16% rate—this 7× performance gap on same offer version strongly suggests tracking breakage, session handling error, or A/B test contamination rather than true user behavior difference. Urgent technical audit required before any copy or creative optimization. Additionally, ga4_supplement is empty (has_custom_events=false), preventing optin page engagement analysis that would help diagnose the landing→optin drop-off issues.
